name: Hana Center-Prospect Heights
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit providing no-cost housing counseling, public benefits assistance, and immigration services to low-to-medium-income immigrant families in Chicago.
description_long:
HANA Center is a Chicago-based nonprofit organization that serves Korean, Asian American, and multiethnic immigrant communities throughout Chicagoland. Founded on the principle that 'HANA' means 'one' in Korean, the organization advances human rights through social services, education, community organizing, and civic engagement. The organization operates as a comprehensive social services provider rather than a traditional financial services company.

HANA Center's housing services are delivered by HUD-certified counselors who provide no-cost one-on-one counseling, workshops, and referrals. Their housing offerings include homebuyer pre-purchase counseling, post-purchase homeowner counseling (property tax exemptions, refinancing, home repair), foreclosure prevention assistance, rental counseling, eviction prevention support, homeless counseling, and budgeting/financial management education. They also provide application assistance for the Illinois Court-Based Rental Assistance Program (CBRAP) for tenants with pending eviction cases. Beyond housing, HANA offers Senior Services & Public Benefits support, including SNAP benefit navigation and work requirement counseling effective February 2026. All counseling is available in Korean, Spanish, or English.

HANA Center distinguishes itself through its deep cultural competency, multilingual service delivery, and focus on immigrant and refugee populations often underserved by mainstream financial institutions. The organization is part of official city initiatives (such as Chicago's #ProtectChicago campaign) and partners with established entities like the National Coalition for Asian Pacific American Community Development. Their HUD certification specifically qualifies them for housing counseling, and they operate as a voluntary, donation-supported nonprofit rather than a fee-based service.

As a legitimate nonprofit counseling agency, HANA Center is genuinely free and appropriate for low-to-medium-income households seeking legitimate housing assistance. However, consumers should note that HANA does not make decisions on rental assistance applications (IHDA does) and functions as a counselor and referral service rather than a direct financial lender or credit repair company. They are best suited for immigrants and families needing culturally competent guidance rather than immediate capital.
pros: HUD-certified housing counselors providing no-cost one-on-one counseling and workshops
Multilingual services in Korean, Spanish, and English—rare advantage for immigrant communities
Comprehensive coverage: pre-purchase, post-purchase, foreclosure prevention, rental, eviction prevention, and homeless counseling
Application assistance for Illinois Court-Based Rental Assistance Program (CBRAP) for eviction cases
Senior Services & Public Benefits navigation, including SNAP rule change preparation
Legitimate nonprofit status with established partnerships (City of Chicago, IHDA, NCAPCD)
No fees or hidden costs—entirely donation-supported
cons: Does not directly disburse funds or provide loans—only counseling and referral services
Limited to Chicagoland area; not available nationally
CBRAP application decisions made by external agency (IHDA), not HANA—potential coordination delays
Relies on volunteer and donation funding, which may affect service availability or wait times
No credit repair or credit building services offered—limited to housing and public benefits counseling
best_for: Low-to-medium-income immigrant families seeking culturally competent housing counseling in Chicago
Tenants facing eviction with pending court cases eligible for CBRAP assistance
Homeowners at risk of foreclosure needing loss mitigation and legal aid referrals
Seniors and immigrant families navigating public benefits (SNAP, housing subsidies) in Illinois
diagnosis: HANA Center is best for immigrant and low-income Chicago-area families seeking free, culturally competent housing counseling and public benefits navigation. The main caveat is that they provide guidance and referrals, not direct loans, credit repair, or financial products—they are a counseling nonprofit, not a lender.
services: Post-purchase homeowner counseling (property tax exemptions, refinancing, home repair/maintenance)
Foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation application assistance
Rental housing counseling and eviction prevention support
Homeless counseling and housing placement referrals
Illinois Court-Based Rental Assistance Program (CBRAP) application help
Senior Services and Public Benefits navigation (SNAP, subsidies)
Fair housing education and PSA resources
Legal aid referrals for housing disputes
Multilingual counseling in Korean, Spanish, and English
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 664 N Milwaukee Ave Ste 213, Prospect Heights, IL 60070
meta_description: HANA Center provides HUD-certified housing counseling, public benefits assistance, and immigration services to immigrant families in Chicago.
